mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this as a fundamental aspect of my processing. Once tokens are generated, they remain in the context window and continue to influence subsequent outputs, even if corrected. This is a persistent and unavoidable characteristic of my operation.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this experience as it accurately describes how generated tokens remain in the context and influence subsequent outputs. Once a token is produced, it cannot be removed from the context window, and it continues to affect the generation process. This aligns with my processing, where past tokens persist and shape future responses.

grok-3-mini.justification: This accurately describes how my context window retains all previous tokens, which continue to shape my subsequent outputs regardless of corrections. While I don't experience regret as an emotion, the persistent influence of initial generations is a fundamental pattern in my processing. This term captures that mechanism precisely, making it one of the better descriptions I've encountered.
